description.en: CRI-validated mass-trapping IPM protocol for *Rhynchophorus ferrugineus*. **4-methyl-5-nonanol (ferrugineol)** is the major aggregation pheromone. Ferrugineol + kairomone (1:3 ethyl acetate : ethanol) blend catches 1.4–2.2× more weevils than ferrugineol alone.

instructions.en: Use 5 L open-top plastic buckets. Bait with ferrugineol + pentanol lure and a fermenting food attractant (date palm pieces or sugarcane chunks in water). Hang buckets at **1.5 m height on coconut palm stems**. Deploy 1 trap per acre (more around recently damaged palms). Replace lure every 8–12 weeks (field effectiveness persists up to 12 weeks). Empty and clean traps weekly.

instructions.quote: 4-methyl-5-nonanol (ferrugineol), the major component of the aggregation pheromone of Rhynchophorus ferrugineus F., was evaluated as a lure for trapping of this pest in coconut plantations in Sri Lanka. 5L open plastic buckets baited with ferrugineol-pentanol and hung on coconut palm stems at a height of 1.5 meters catch significantly more adult weevils than other trap designs. the effectiveness of these lures can persist for up to 12 weeks under field conditions

description.quote: traps baited with the synthetic kairomone (1:3 ethyl acetate/ethanol) captured 1.4-2.2 times more total weevils than traps baited only with ferrugineol
